title: Counterfactual explanations without opening the black box: Automated decisions and the GDPR
methodology: Legal-technical analysis of GDPR Articles 13-15 and 22 + algorithmic definition of counterfactual explanation.
contributions: The 'right to explanation' read into GDPR Article 22 is more meaningfully discharged by counterfactual explanations than by feature-attribution explanations.
Counterfactuals require no access to the model internals — model-agnostic by construction.
A formal definition of counterfactual: the closest possible world (in feature space) in which the decision would have differed.
weaknesses: The 'right to explanation' reading of GDPR is contested (Edwards & Veale 2017, Selbst & Powles 2017 argue it is more limited).
Closest counterfactual is not always the most useful — Mothilal et al. (2020) argue for diversity.
Predates the EU AI Act; new framework will partly supersede GDPR analysis for credit-scoring AI.
emerald_relevance: Legal foundation citation for EMERALD-AI's counterfactual layer. Justifies the design choice to make counterfactuals a first-class output, not an optional add-on.
search_query: Wachter Mittelstadt Russell counterfactual explanations GDPR HJLT 2017
notes: Foundational legal/technical argument for counterfactuals under the right to explanation.
